html:
Tage Fritjof Erlander, born on June 13, 1901, in Ransäter, Värmland County, and died on June 21, 1985, in Stockholm, was the leader of the Swedish Social Democratic Labour Party and served as Prime Minister of Sweden from 1946 to 1969. With over 23 years in office, Tage Erlander held the position of head of government for a longer continuous period than any other leader among all parliamentary democracies worldwide. He was a member of the Swedish Parliament (Riksdag) from 1932 to 1973.

== Early Life ==

Erlander was the son of schoolteacher and choir director Erik Gustaf Erlander (1859–1936) and Alma Nilsson (1869–1961). He was the third of four children; his siblings were Janne (1893–1912), Anna (1894–1972), and Dagmar (1904–1988). Erlander's family on his maternal grandmother's side descended from what is known as a "svedjefinne" (a group of people who practiced slash-and-burn agriculture). Tage Erlander's father was originally named Andersson, after his own father, Anders Erlandsson, but he changed the name to Erlander. Erlander was born on June 13, 1901, on the top floor of the house that is now called Erlandergården. His family lived on that floor for much of his childhood, and he attended school in the building's lower floors.

== Education ==

Erlander graduated from the Karlstad Higher General School in 1920 and enrolled at Lund University in the autumn of the same year. At Lund, Tage Erlander initially studied physics and chemistry under professors like Manne Siegbahn. He interrupted his studies for mandatory military service in the First Life Grenadier Regiment (I 4) from 1921 to 1922, serving in the machine gun company in Linköping. Upon returning to his studies, Erlander became chairman of the newly established Lund Mathematical Society in 1923, where he also met his future wife, Aina Andersson.

During the spring of 1925, Erlander's interest in physics gradually waned. In 1927, he passed his exams in political science, and in the summer of 1928, he took exams in economics and statistics. In September 1928, he earned his bachelor's degree based on these three subjects.

Erlander became involved with social democracy during his studies at Lund. It was there that he embraced this ideology, although it wasn't until 1928 that he officially joined the party. In his memoirs, he emphasizes the brutality of the police force during the agricultural worker strikes outside Lund in 1923 as a shocking experience that shaped his political worldview and led him to abandon the radical liberalism he had previously held.

He was active in student politics, serving as chairman of the culturally progressive student association "De Yngre Gubbarna" (D.Y.G.), and along with his fiancée Aina, he was also a member of the left-wing organization Clarté. He was also involved in activities at his student nation, serving as curator for Wermlands Nation from 1926 to 1929; in this role, he helped establish the Curators' College in 1927 and the Grand Curators' College in 1928.

After earning his bachelor's degree in 1928, he was employed at Svensk Uppslagsbok (Swedish Encyclopedia) in Malmö that same year, where he became an editorial secretary and served on the editorial committee from volume number 7 in 1932–1938.

== Early Political Positions ==

In 1930, Erlander was elected to the city council of Lund, and in 1932, he became a member of the second chamber of parliament, representing the four-district constituency. In 1937, he became an expert at the Ministry of Social Affairs under Gustav Möller, and in 1938, he was appointed as a state secretary.

As a state secretary at the Ministry of Social Affairs, Erlander was one of the most responsible individuals for the 14 secret internment camps in Sweden during World War II. Erlander was deeply involved in the establishment and operation of these camps. He later wrote about them in his memoirs, which were published in the 1970s, but the author Niclas Sennerteg stated that, upon publication of the book "Swedish Concentration Camps in the Shadow of the Third Reich" (2008), that Erlander's portrayal of events in the memoirs did not accurately reflect reality. Sennerteg argued that Erlander "received regular information about what was happening in the camps long before he claimed."

== Minister == In 1944, Erlander was appointed as a consultative minister (deputy minister of social affairs) responsible for issues related to the labor market, police force, and civil defense within the coalition government led by Per Albin Hansson. The following year, he became the Minister of Ecclesiastical Affairs when the coalition government dissolved and a Social Democratic one-party government was formed.

== Party Leader and Prime Minister == When the former Prime Minister Per Albin Hansson unexpectedly died in October 1946, Erlander was surprisingly elected as the new party leader and thus became Prime Minister. The competition with the then Minister of Social Affairs Gustav Möller for the party leadership was intense, but Erlander, who himself was initially reluctant to become party leader, had the most support both within the party board and in the parliamentary group. At the time of his appointment, Erlander was virtually unknown to the public. Many believed that he would resign after the 1948 second chamber election because he was largely unknown and it would be difficult for him to fill Hansson's shoes.

Erlander had a greater impact on Sweden's development in the decades following World War II than any other politician. As party leader, after his initial success in the 1948 election, Erlander held an unchallenged position based on his combination of pragmatism and...

How certain are we about this?

Information about people from the 20th century is extremely well-documented. Uncertainty rarely concerns facts themselves, but rather interpretations and evaluations, which can change over time and depending on the perspective.
tldr: Tage Erlander served as the Prime Minister of Sweden from 1946 to 1969. He held office longer than any other democratically elected leader in the world during that period.
lattlast:
Tage Erlander was born in 1901 in Värmland. He studied at Lund University, where he initially pursued natural sciences but later switched to political science and economics. It was during these studies that he became a member of the Social Democratic Party.

Before becoming Prime Minister, he worked in politics at various levels. For example, he served as a state secretary, a high-ranking position within a government agency or department. During World War II, he held significant responsibility for the operation of certain secret facilities in Sweden.

In 1946, he was elected leader of the party and Prime Minister after the previous leader's death. Despite many believing that he would struggle to gain public support, he became a highly influential leader who governed Sweden for a long period after World War II.
